brief_document.title: WorkflowContinuedAsNew event and ContinuedAsNew terminal status
brief_document.purpose: Add the core domain types for continue-as-new: the WorkflowContinuedAsNew event variant and the ContinuedAsNew terminal workflow status. These are the foundation every other AH brief builds on. The event carries the input payload for the new run, an optional workflow type override for migration, and the parent_run_id linking to the previous run. The status is derived by projection, consistent with the status-is-a-projection invariant.
brief_document.task: Add a WorkflowContinuedAsNew variant to the Event enum in aion-core carrying an EventEnvelope, the carried Payload (new run input), an optional workflow_type override (String), and parent_run_id (RunId). Add ContinuedAsNew to the WorkflowStatus enum as a terminal status. Update the status projection (status_of) to derive ContinuedAsNew from WorkflowContinuedAsNew events. Update all exhaustive matches on Event and WorkflowStatus across aion-core (filter, is_terminal, display, serde) to handle the new variants.

requirements.title: WorkflowContinuedAsNew event variant
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add a WorkflowContinuedAsNew variant to the Event enum carrying: an EventEnvelope, an input Payload (the carried state for the new run), an optional workflow_type (Option<String>) for migration, and a parent_run_id (RunId) linking to the current run that is being continued.
acceptance: Event::WorkflowContinuedAsNew exists with fields envelope: EventEnvelope, input: Payload, workflow_type: Option<String>, parent_run_id: RunId.
The variant serializes and deserializes correctly via serde (round-trip test).
The variant is handled in every exhaustive match on Event in aion-core (filter.rs, status.rs, and any other match sites).

requirements.title: ContinuedAsNew terminal status
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add ContinuedAsNew to the WorkflowStatus enum. WorkflowStatus::ContinuedAsNew SHALL be terminal (is_terminal returns true). The status_of projection SHALL derive ContinuedAsNew from WorkflowContinuedAsNew events.
acceptance: status_of applied to a history ending with WorkflowContinuedAsNew returns Some(WorkflowStatus::ContinuedAsNew).
A history with WorkflowStarted then WorkflowContinuedAsNew projects to ContinuedAsNew status.
All exhaustive matches on WorkflowStatus in aion-core handle the new variant.

requirements.title: Filter and projection integration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL treat WorkflowContinuedAsNew as a terminal lifecycle event in all filter and projection paths. THE SYSTEM SHALL include it in terminal-event filters and closed_at extraction alongside WorkflowCompleted, WorkflowFailed, WorkflowCancelled, and WorkflowTimedOut.
acceptance: closed_at extraction returns the envelope's recorded_at for WorkflowContinuedAsNew.
cargo clippy -p aion-core --all-targets -- -D warnings passes with no unhandled-variant warnings.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the Recorder recording path — that is AH-002.
SHALL NOT implement the Engine::continue_as_new method — that is AH-003.
SHALL NOT modify the EventStore trait or any store implementation — that is AH-002.
SHALL NOT add SDK bindings — those are a later brief.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

constraints.text: ContinuedAsNew is a terminal status with exactly one corresponding terminal event (WorkflowContinuedAsNew), consistent with the status-is-a-projection invariant.
The new run's WorkflowStarted must carry parent_run_id so the continuation chain is navigable.
Continue-as-new with pending activities or children is a hard error, not auto-cancellation.
The carried payload is subject to the same size constraints as any workflow input.
Recovery (list_active + replay) must never attempt to replay a ContinuedAsNew run.
The engine must start the new run atomically with completing the old run — no window where neither exists.

resolved_context.intention: Long-running workflows accumulate unbounded event histories. A workflow that loops forever — processing orders, monitoring a sensor, managing a subscription — grows its event log with every iteration. Replay on recovery re-executes every recorded step from the beginning, so performance degrades linearly with history size. Without a mechanism to bound history, Aion cannot support long-lived workflows in production. This cluster adds continue-as-new: a workflow explicitly restarts itself with a fresh event log, carrying forward only the state it needs. The old execution completes as ContinuedAsNew (a terminal status), and a new execution begins immediately with the carried-over state as input. Replay of the new execution starts from its own WorkflowStarted event, not from the original. This is the same mechanism Temporal uses and it is the only sound approach — snapshotting with truncation would require the snapshot to be replay-correct, which is equivalent to re-executing anyway.

provenance.quote: Long-running workflows accumulate unbounded event histories. A workflow that loops forever — processing orders, monitoring a sensor, managing a subscription — grows its event log with every iteration.
